questions.question: A developer needs to classify 50,000 support tickets per day with sub-200ms latency. Which Claude model tier is most appropriate?
options: Haiku — optimized for speed and high-volume, low-latency tasks
Sonnet — the balanced model handles most tasks efficiently
Opus — highest capability ensures accurate classification
Any tier works equally; latency is controlled via streaming, not model choice
questions.explanation: Haiku is the low-latency, high-volume tier. Sonnet and Opus are slower and more expensive; the task complexity here doesn't justify them.

questions.question: What happens if you omit the required `max_tokens` parameter from a Messages API request?
options: The request fails with a 400 invalid_request_error
Claude uses a default of 1,024 output tokens
Claude uses the model's full context window as the output limit
The request succeeds but output may be silently truncated
questions.explanation: max_tokens is required. Omitting it returns a 400 invalid_request_error immediately — unlike some other APIs, there is no default.

questions.question: A response returns `stop_reason: "max_tokens"`. What does this mean for the response content?
options: The response was truncated — Claude stopped at the token limit, not at a natural end
The response completed normally and happened to use exactly the max_tokens budget
Claude stopped for safety reasons and the response may be incomplete
The context window is full; the conversation must be reset before continuing
questions.explanation: stop_reason "max_tokens" means output was cut off mid-generation. The response may be incomplete. Increase max_tokens or redesign the prompt to produce shorter output.

questions.question: You want Claude to always start its response with `{` to produce valid JSON. What technique achieves this most reliably?
options: Assistant prefilling — add an assistant message with content: "{" as the last item in the messages array
Set `response_format: {"type": "json_object"}` in the request body
End your system prompt with: "Begin every response with the character {"
Use `stop_sequences: ["}"]` to bookend the JSON output
questions.explanation: Prefilling the assistant message with "{" forces Claude to continue from that exact character. The response_format parameter used in other APIs does not exist in the Claude API.

questions.question: What is the context window size for all current Claude models (Haiku, Sonnet, Opus)?
options: 200,000 tokens for all current Claude models
100,000 tokens for Haiku, 200,000 for Sonnet and Opus
128,000 tokens for all current models
32k tokens (Haiku), 100k (Sonnet), 200k (Opus)
questions.explanation: All current Claude models share a 200,000-token context window covering the combined input and output tokens in a single request.

questions.question: The API returns a 429 error. What is the correct response from your application?
options: Retry with exponential backoff — most SDKs handle this automatically
Switch to a lower-tier model immediately to reduce load on Anthropic's servers
Reduce max_tokens and retry immediately without waiting
Pause for 24 hours and retry; 429s indicate a daily quota breach
questions.explanation: 429 is a rate-limit error, not a daily quota. Retry with exponential backoff. The Anthropic Python and TypeScript SDKs handle automatic retries by default.

questions.question: You send a conversation with the message sequence: user → user → assistant. What happens?
options: The request fails with a 400 error — messages must alternate between user and assistant roles
Succeeds — Claude concatenates the two consecutive user messages into one turn
Succeeds — the first user message is silently ignored and only the second is processed
Fails with a 422 unprocessable entity error listing the invalid role sequence
questions.explanation: Messages must strictly alternate between user and assistant roles. Two consecutive user messages cause a 400 invalid_request_error.

questions.question: Approximately how many tokens is one average English word?
options: ~1.3 tokens per word (roughly 3–4 characters per token)
Exactly 1 token per word
~2 tokens per word
~0.75 tokens per word
questions.explanation: Roughly 1.3 tokens per word, so 1,000 tokens ≈ 750 words. Technical or uncommon words tokenize to more; common short words often to fewer.

questions.question: You want to ensure your application doesn't break when Anthropic releases a new version of Sonnet. What should you do?
options: Pin to a specific dated model ID (e.g., `claude-sonnet-4-6`) rather than an undated alias
Use the undated alias `claude-sonnet` — it resolves to the most stable available version
Set `"model_lock": true` in your API request parameters
Monitor Anthropic's changelog and manually update the model ID after each release
questions.explanation: Undated aliases always point to the latest model release and can change behavior without warning. Pinning to a full, dated model ID gives you stable, predictable outputs.

questions.question: Which parameter controls the randomness of Claude's output?
options: temperature (0–1 scale; lower = more deterministic)
top_p (nucleus sampling threshold — the primary randomness control)
frequency_penalty (penalizes repeated tokens)
seed (makes outputs reproducible across identical calls)
questions.explanation: temperature is the primary randomness control. top_p is an alternative sampling strategy; don't use both together. frequency_penalty and seed are not standard Claude API parameters.

questions.question: Claude returns a response with two content blocks: one `text` and one `tool_use`. What should your application do?
options: Handle both — optionally display the text and always execute the tool_use block
Only process the tool_use block; text blocks alongside tool_use are informational metadata
Return an error — Claude should produce either text or tool_use, never both
Process whichever block appears last in the content array
questions.explanation: Both blocks must be handled. The text often explains what Claude is doing; the tool_use block must always receive a tool_result response before generation can continue.

questions.question: What is the purpose of the `metadata.user_id` field in a Messages API request?
options: Logging and analytics; Anthropic may also use it for safety monitoring
Selects which user profile Claude personalizes responses for
Required for multi-user deployments to prevent conversation context mixing
Determines which rate-limit bucket the request is counted against
questions.explanation: metadata.user_id is optional and has no effect on Claude's response. It helps you with logging and gives Anthropic signal for abuse detection.

questions.question: You need Claude to stop generating as soon as it writes the string `END`. How do you achieve this?
options: Set `stop_sequences: ["END"]` in the request — the string will not appear in the output
Set `end_token: "END"` in the request body
Add "Stop generating when you output the word END" to your system prompt
Use `suffix: "END"` to mark the end of the desired output
questions.explanation: stop_sequences causes Claude to halt when it would produce that string. The string itself is consumed and does not appear in the response content.

questions.question: The API returns a 529 error. What caused it and how should you respond?
options: Anthropic's servers are temporarily overloaded — retry with exponential backoff
Your API key is invalid — check and rotate credentials immediately
The request payload exceeds the maximum size — reduce your prompt length
The requested model is unavailable in your region
questions.explanation: 529 overloaded_error means the API is under heavy load. Handle it the same way as a 429: retry with exponential backoff.

questions.question: The token counting endpoint returns 195,000 input tokens. You plan to allow up to 8,000 output tokens on a model with a 200,000-token context window. Is this request safe to send?
options: No — 195,000 + 8,000 = 203,000 tokens exceeds the 200,000-token context window
Yes — max_tokens caps the output but is not counted against the context window
Yes — output tokens are tracked separately from input tokens in the context limit
Yes — the 200k limit applies only to the messages array, not the system prompt
questions.explanation: Input tokens and output tokens both count toward the context window. 195k + 8k = 203k, which exceeds the 200k limit. You must reduce the input or lower max_tokens.
